Name: The Seventy Translators
Significance: Created the Septuagint, Greek translation of Hebrew Bible

Name: Antigonus of Sokho
Significance: Early Pharisaic figure; first Jew known to have a Greek name.

Name: Demetrius the Chronographer
Significance: Wrote on biblical chronology in Alexandria

Name: Aristobulus of Alexandria
Significance: Attempted to fuse Hebrew Scriptures with Greek thought.

Name: Jason/Joshua
Significance: Hellenizing high priest who promoted Greek customs in Jerusalem

Name: Aristobulus of Paneas
Significance: Early Jewish philosopher who interpreted Torah allegorically

Name: Menelaus
Significance: Extreme Hellenizer who supported Antiochus IV's policies

Name: Alcimus
Significance: Hellenized high priest during the Maccabean period

Name: Eupolemus
Significance: Jewish historian who wrote in Greek about Jewish history

Name: Ezekiel the Tragedian
Significance: Wrote Greek tragedies based on biblical themes

Name: Artapanus
Significance: Wrote about biblical figures in Greek cultural terms

Name: Jason of Cyrene
Significance: Author whose work was summarized in 2 Maccabees

Name: Salome Alexandra
Significance: Queen of Judea who navigated between Pharisaic Judaism and Hellenistic politics

Name: Simon ben Shetach
Significance: Pharisaic sage who incorporated Greek educational methods while maintaining Jewish law

Name: Judah Aristobulus I
Significance: First Hasmonean to call himself king, balancing Jewish tradition with Hellenistic kingship

Name: Boethus
Significance: Founder of the Boethusians, a Hellenized Sadducean sect

Name: Cleodemus Malchus
Significance: Connected Jewish and Greek mythologies

Name: Philo of Alexandria
Significance: Synthesized Jewish theology with Greek philosophy

Name: Alexander the Alabarch
Significance: Wealthy Alexandrian Jew, customs official, brother of Philo

Name: Agrippa I
Significance: Grandson of Herod who ruled as a Hellenized Jewish king with connections to Rome

Name: Tiberius Julius Alexander
Significance: Nephew of Philo who abandoned Judaism for Roman service.

Name: Dositheus the Samaritan
Significance: Founded a Hellenized Samaritan sect influenced by Greek thought

Name: Ananus ben Ananus
Significance: Hellenized high priest who represented Jewish religious authority to Rome

Name: Agrippa II
Significance: Last Herodian king who embodied Hellenistic Jewish royalty under Roman authority

Name: Justus of Tiberias
Significance: Rival to Josephus; chronicled Jewish history from a Hellenistic perspective.

Name: Josephus
Significance: Wrote Jewish history in Greek for Roman audience

Name: Pseudo-Phocylides
Significance: Author of moral poetry blending Jewish ethics with Greek forms

Name: Nicolaus of Damascus
Significance: Greek historian who served as advisor to Herod the Great and wrote his biography

Name: Lukuas (Andreas)
Significance: Led Jewish resistance during the Diaspora Revolt in North Africa and Egypt.

Name: Herod the Great
Significance: Hellenized Jewish king under Roman patronage

Name: Julian and Pappos
Significance: Leaders of Jewish resistance during the Kitos War against Rome.

Name: Trypho the Jew
Significance: Engaged in debates with early Christians; influenced by both Greek and Rabbinic thought.
